howItWorks.intro: This diaper size calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Diaper sizes overlap in weight ranges for a comfortable fit
Average cost: $0.20-0.35 per diaper depending on brand
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How do I know when to size up?
faq.answer: Signs you need the next size: frequent leaks or blowouts, red marks on thighs or waist, difficulty fastening tabs, the diaper looks too small. It's better to size up early than deal with leaks.

faq.question: How many diapers does a baby use?
faq.answer: Newborn: 10-12/day. 3 months: 8-10/day. 6 months: 7-8/day. 12 months: 6-7/day. Toddler: 5-6/day. That's roughly 2,500-3,000 diapers in the first year, costing $600-900.

faq.question: Are store brand diapers as good as name brand?
faq.answer: Many parents find store brands (Up&Up, Parent's Choice, Kirkland) work just as well at 40-50% less cost. Premium brands may fit better for some body types. Try a small pack before committing to a large box.
